title: Retrieval Quiz
description: Students answer short questions to recall prior knowledge before the new lesson content begins.
teacherRole: Prepare focused questions and review answers quickly.
studentTask: Answer questions individually and correct responses during feedback.

title: Think-Pair-Share
description: Students think independently about a prompt, discuss with a partner, then share ideas with the class.
teacherRole: Pose a clear question and guide short feedback.
studentTask: Think, discuss, and share responses.

title: Guided Investigation
description: Students investigate a question using structured steps, collect observations, and draw conclusions.
teacherRole: Set up the task, support safe investigation, and question students during the process.
studentTask: Carry out the investigation, record results, and explain findings.

title: Teacher Demonstration
description: The teacher demonstrates a process or concept while students observe, predict, and explain what happens.
teacherRole: Model the process clearly and ask guiding questions.
studentTask: Observe, answer questions, and record key points.

title: Application Task
description: Students apply the lesson concept to short scenarios, questions, or real-world examples.
teacherRole: Provide tasks and support students who need guidance.
studentTask: Complete the task and explain reasoning.

title: Exit Ticket
description: Students answer one or two final questions before leaving to show what they understood.
teacherRole: Collect responses and identify misconceptions.
studentTask: Answer the final prompt individually.

title: Carousel Brainstorm
description: Students work in small groups and rotate between prompts, adding ideas to each station before reviewing the class responses.
teacherRole: Prepare prompts, manage timing, and guide students to compare and prioritise ideas.
studentTask: Add original ideas at each station and review contributions from other groups.

title: Circular Brainstorm
description: Students sit in a circle and each contributes one idea or response to a shared question or problem.
teacherRole: Introduce the question, record key ideas, and support respectful listening.
studentTask: Think quietly, contribute one idea, and listen to the ideas of others.

title: Think-Pair-Share-Snowball
description: Students first think individually, then discuss with a partner, then combine ideas with a larger group before sharing with the class.
teacherRole: Pose an open-ended question, manage each stage, and draw out deeper reasoning.
studentTask: Develop an individual response, refine it through discussion, and contribute to a group answer.

title: Walking Debate
description: Students physically position themselves according to whether they agree, disagree, or are unsure about a statement, then explain their reasoning.
teacherRole: Read statements, invite evidence-based explanations, and allow students to change position as they hear new perspectives.
studentTask: Choose a position, justify it, listen to others, and reconsider their view if needed.

title: Power of Persuasion
description: Students choose from several possible answers to a question, form groups, and attempt to persuade others to support their position.
teacherRole: Frame the question, chair the campaign discussion, and support students in using evidence.
studentTask: Develop arguments, present a position, respond to others, and reflect on what was persuasive.

title: Hot Seat
description: One student or group takes on a role or viewpoint while the rest of the class prepares and asks questions.
teacherRole: Assign roles, support respectful questioning, and help students connect answers to the lesson focus.
studentTask: Prepare questions or respond in role using evidence and reasoning.

title: Problem-Based Learning Task
description: Students investigate an open-ended problem where they must define the issue, explore possible solutions, and justify their decisions.
teacherRole: Present the problem, support investigation, and prompt students to explain their reasoning.
studentTask: Analyse the problem, identify possible solutions, and defend their chosen approach.

title: Jigsaw Learning
description: Students become experts on one subtopic, then reorganise into mixed groups where each student teaches their part to others.
teacherRole: Divide the topic into subtopics, organise expert groups, and support accurate peer teaching.
studentTask: Research one subtopic, prepare an explanation, and teach it to classmates.

title: Placemat Activity
description: Students write individual ideas in separate sections of a shared page, discuss them as a group, and place the strongest ideas in the centre.
teacherRole: Set a clear task, monitor group discussion, and guide students to prioritise ideas.
studentTask: Write individual ideas, discuss with the group, and agree on the most important shared points.

title: Peer Teaching
description: Students explain a concept or process to a peer, using examples, diagrams, or step-by-step reasoning.
teacherRole: Pair students strategically, provide guidance, and check the accuracy of explanations.
studentTask: Explain the concept clearly to a peer and ask or answer questions.

title: Case Study Scenario
description: Students analyse a fictional or real-world scenario and apply lesson concepts to explain, advise, or solve a problem.
teacherRole: Provide the scenario, guide discussion, and connect responses back to the learning outcomes.
studentTask: Read the scenario, identify the issue, and propose a reasoned response or solution.

title: Visual Media Analysis
description: Students examine an image, diagram, photograph, or short video and discuss what it shows, what it suggests, and what questions it raises.
teacherRole: Select suitable visual material and prompt students to question, compare, and justify interpretations.
studentTask: Observe carefully, identify key details, and explain interpretations using evidence.

title: Reflective Writing
description: Students write a short reflection on what they learned, what changed in their thinking, or what they still need to understand.
teacherRole: Provide a focused reflection prompt and use responses to identify next steps.
studentTask: Write a short individual reflection linked to the lesson focus.

title: Quiet Time Reflection
description: Students take a short quiet moment to reflect on the lesson, focus their attention, and consider one personal takeaway.
teacherRole: Guide a calm reflection and give students time to return to the present before closing.
studentTask: Listen, reflect quietly, and identify one takeaway from the lesson.
